subject: Economics
paper_name: Edexcel Economics A Paper 2: The National and Global Economy (June 2022)

sections.name: Section A: Data Response — UK Government Spending
Section B: Data Response — Emerging Economies

questions.question_text: With reference to Extract A, identify two significant features of the data shown.
questions.extract_text: Extract A shows UK government spending and national debt from 2018 to 2022. Total government spending rose from £842bn (39% of GDP) in 2018/19 to £1,047bn (44% of GDP) in 2021/22, before falling to £1,029bn (43% of GDP) in 2022/23. National debt increased from £1.8 trillion (84% of GDP) to £2.5 trillion (100% of GDP) over the same period. Debt interest payments reached £111bn in 2022/23, up from £38bn in 2019/20.

questions.question_text: With reference to the information provided, explain the difference between a government budget deficit and the national debt, and why both are significant for macroeconomic stability.
With reference to Extract B and your own knowledge, assess the view that austerity measures are necessary to reduce the national debt.
questions.extract_text: Extract B: Following the COVID-19 pandemic, the UK government spent £370bn on emergency support measures. The IMF recommended that the UK pursue a 'credible medium-term fiscal consolidation' strategy. However, the OECD noted that premature austerity following the 2008 financial crisis had slowed recovery in several European countries. The UK's debt-to-GDP ratio at 100% compares to Japan (263%), Italy (145%), and the US (123%). Some economists argue that with historically low interest rates in recent years, governments should have borrowed more for productive investment.

questions.question_text: With reference to the extracts and your own knowledge, evaluate the effectiveness of automatic stabilisers versus discretionary fiscal policy in managing the economic cycle.
Evaluate the view that the government should prioritise reducing the budget deficit over promoting economic growth.
With reference to Extract C, identify two significant features of the data shown.
questions.extract_text: Extract C shows economic indicators for selected emerging economies in 2022. India's GDP growth was 6.8%, compared to 3.0% for China and 2.9% for Brazil. India's GDP per capita (PPP) was $8,379 compared to China's $21,476. Foreign direct investment into India reached $84bn in 2022, while FDI into China fell by 48% to $180bn. India's services sector accounted for 53% of GDP, while manufacturing represented only 17%, compared to 28% in China.

questions.question_text: With reference to the information provided, explain how foreign direct investment can promote economic development in emerging economies.
With reference to Extract D and your own knowledge, assess the extent to which globalisation has benefited developing and emerging economies.
questions.extract_text: Extract D: Global trade as a percentage of world GDP peaked at 61% in 2008 and has since fallen to 57%. Multinational corporations account for approximately 80% of world trade. The UNCTAD World Investment Report 2022 noted that while FDI has raised productivity and employment in many developing countries, the benefits have been unevenly distributed. Countries with strong institutions and human capital — such as Vietnam, India and Bangladesh — have attracted more FDI than Sub-Saharan African nations. Critics argue that MNC profits are often repatriated rather than reinvested, and that developing countries are locked into low-value stages of global supply chains.

questions.question_text: With reference to the extracts and your own knowledge, evaluate the role of the World Trade Organisation in promoting economic development.
Evaluate the view that protectionist trade policies can be justified for developing and emerging economies.
Evaluate the view that demand-side policies alone are sufficient to achieve sustained economic growth and full employment. Refer to at least one diagram in your answer.
Evaluate the extent to which inequality of income and wealth is an inevitable consequence of a market economy. Refer to at least one diagram in your answer.
